title: chat_using() OnceLock is overengineered for what it does
description: chat_using() returns &'static [String] via OnceLock<Vec<String>> to avoid allocating the two capability URI strings on every request. But build_request() calls .to_vec() on the result anyway, so we're paying for two heap allocations per call regardless. The OnceLock saves the initial String allocs but adds static state and a lazy-init pattern that has to be understood and maintained. Simpler: just return the vec directly. Two short strings per request is noise.
